keyPointsSummary: 'Had to' is the past tense of has to/have to — same form for all subjects
'Will have to' expresses future obligation — same form for all subjects
Negative past: did not have to; Negative future: will not have to
Question: Did + subject + have to...? / Will + subject + have to...?

quickQuiz.answer: I had to go to office yesterday.
I will have to go to office tomorrow.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसे यह काम पूरा करना पड़ा।
quickQuiz.answer: He had to complete this work.

quickQuiz.hindi: उसे यह काम पूरा करना पड़ेगा।
quickQuiz.answer: He will have to complete this work.

quickQuiz.hindi: हमें नियमों का पालन करना पड़ा।
quickQuiz.answer: We had to follow the rules.

quickQuiz.hindi: हमें नियमों का पालन करना पड़ेगा।
quickQuiz.answer: We will have to follow the rules.
She had to wake up early every day.
She will have to wake up early every day.
Did you have to do this?
Will you have to do this?

mustRememberRules: 'Had to' is the past tense of has to/have to — same form for all subjects
'Will have to' expresses future obligation — same form for all subjects
Negative past: did not have to; Negative future: will not have to
Question: Did + subject + have to...? / Will + subject + have to...?
